perturbation.original_text: 5.3 Royalty/Commission Payments. Dr. Murray will receive an annual royalty on net sales (defined as gross sales minus returns) for any products (the "Dr. Murray Products") developed by Dr. Murray for EHN for as long as the Dr. Murray Products are being sold. The Dr. Murray Products will be listed on Schedule A attached hereto as they are developed and added to product portfolio. During each year of this agreement, Dr. Murray will be paid no later than the 30st day of January based on the cumulative Net Sales of the Dr. Murray Products for the preceding 12 months based upon the following scale: Net Sales per 12 Months Royalty $2,000,001 to $3,500,000 $150,000 $3,500,001 to $5,000,000 $250,000 $5,000,001 to $10,000,000 $350,000 $10,000,001 to $25,000,000 $500,000 $25,000,001 to $50,000,000 $1,000,000 $50,000,001 to $75,000,000 $1,500,000 $75,000,001 to $100,000,000 $2,000,000 Greater than $100,000,000 $2,500,000
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 Royalty/Commission Payments. Dr. Murray will receive an annual royalty on net sales (defined as gross sales minus returns) for any products (the "Dr. Murray Products") developed by Dr. Murray for EHN for as long as the Dr. Murray Products are being sold. The Dr. Murray Products will be listed on Schedule A attached hereto as they are developed and added to product portfolio. During each year of this agreement, Dr. Murray will be paid no later than the 30st day of January based on the cumulative Net Sales of the Dr. Murray Products for the preceding 12 months.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the royalty payment scale, the section now references a royalty payment without specifying the amount or how it's calculated. This creates an in-text contradiction because the text implies a payment structure exists but provides no details, rendering the 'royalty' obligation unenforceable due to lack of clarity. The text continues to state Dr. Murray will be paid, but not how much he will be paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3 Royalty/Commission Payments. Dr. Murray will receive an annual royalty on net sales (defined as gross sales minus returns) for any products (the "Dr. Murray Products") developed by Dr. Murray for EHN for as long as the Dr. Murray Products are being sold. The Dr. Murray Products will be listed on Schedule A attached hereto as they are developed and added to product portfolio. During each year of this agreement, Dr. Murray will be paid no later than the 30st day of January based on the cumulative Net Sales of the Dr. Murray Products for the preceding 12 months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the royalty payment scale previously defined in section 5.3. This creates an in-text contradiction with the rest of section 5.3 which still refers to a royalty payment based on net sales but provides no mechanism for determining the amount, making the obligation unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE 7 -CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Neither EHS, EHN nor Dr. Murray shall disclose to any third parties, except as required by law, at any time during or subsequent to the term of this Agreement, any Confidential Information. "Confidential Information: includes proprietary information, technical data, trade secrets or know-how, including, but not limited to, the terms and conditions of this Agreement, research, product plans, products, services, suppliers, customer lists and customers, prices and costs, markets, inventions, technology, formulas, specifications, designs, drawings, marketing, licenses, finances, budgets and other business information. Confidential information does not include information that (i) is or becomes generally known to the public, through no wrongful act of the receiving party; (ii) is lawfully obtained by the receiving party from a third party which has no obligation to maintain the information as confidential; (iii) was known prior to its disclosure to the receiving party without any obligation to keep it confidential as evidenced by tangible records kept by the receiving party 3 Source: EMERALD HEALTH BIOCEUTICALS INC., 1-A, 2/18/2020 in the ordinary course of its business; (iv) is independently developed by the receiving party without reference to the disclosing party's Confidential Information; or (v) is the subject of a written agreement whereby the disclosing party consents to the use or disclosure of such Confidential Information. If the disclosure of any such confidential information by EHS or Dr. Murray to any third party is required in order to carry out the purpose and intent of this Agreement, then EHS and Dr. Murray shall cause such third party to enter into a non-disclosure Agreement with EHS and/or Dr. Murray as a condition to such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE 7 -CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Neither EHS, EHN nor Dr. Murray shall disclose to any third parties, except as required by law, at any time during or subsequent to the term of this Agreement, any Confidential Information. If the disclosure of any such confidential information by EHS or Dr. Murray to any third party is required in order to carry out the purpose and intent of this Agreement, then EHS and Dr. Murray shall cause such third party to enter into a non-disclosure Agreement with EHS and/or Dr. Murray as a condition to such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the definition of 'Confidential Information' from Article 7 creates an in-text contradiction. The text still prohibits the disclosure of 'Confidential Information,' but without a definition, it is unclear what information is protected. The clause requiring a non-disclosure agreement for third parties becomes ambiguous because the subject of that agreement (Confidential Information) is undefined. Therefore, the article loses enforceability, but maintains the prohibition of disclosure and requirement for non-disclosure agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE 7 -CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Neither EHS, EHN nor Dr. Murray shall disclose to any third parties, except as required by law, at any time during or subsequent to the term of this Agreement, any Confidential Information. If the disclosure of any such confidential information by EHS or Dr. Murray to any third party is required in order to carry out the purpose and intent of this Agreement, then EHS and Dr. Murray shall cause such third party to enter into a non-disclosure Agreement with EHS and/or Dr. Murray as a condition to such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the definition of 'Confidential Information,' while still referencing and prohibiting its disclosure. The contradicted text is the changed text. However, since the original text contained the definition, and that definition was removed, this creates a clear contradiction. Without a definition, the provision requiring non-disclosure agreements becomes unenforceable and the general prohibition on disclosing 'Confidential Information' is rendered meaningless.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2 Options. Upon execution of this Agreement and on each anniversary date of this Agreement for as long as this Agreement is active, EHS will grant Dr. Murray options to purchase 25,000 shares of EHS common stock at their then fair market value (the "Options"). The Options will vest immediately on the date of grant.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2 Options. Upon execution of this Agreement and on each anniversary date of this Agreement for as long as this Agreement is active, EHS will grant Dr. Murray options.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the information about the number of shares and their value, EHS is obligated to grant options but there is no description of what these options are. While Section 5.2 refers to the provision of options, the removal of the quantity and value associated with those options creates an internal contradiction. The contract maintains the obligation to grant options, but the specifics of what those options entail are omitted. This creates uncertainty because the text references 'options', but there is no description associated with these options in the altered text, and no way for Dr. Murray to enforce the granting of options because there is no way of knowing what the options actually are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Options. Upon execution of this Agreement and on each anniversary date of this Agreement for as long as this Agreement is active, EHS will grant Dr. Murray options.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specifics of the options granted to Dr. Murray (number of shares, fair market value). While the changed text and the contradicted text are identical, the omission of key details regarding the options granted in the changed text directly contradicts the original intent and enforceability of the agreement. Without defining the quantity and value, the obligation to grant options becomes vague and unenforceable. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction regarding obligations stated elsewhere (specifically, the implied obligation to grant options with defined terms).
